'''Ethan Havard''' (born 26 October 2000) is an {{rlnt|England}} international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]]. He has also played for the [[England Knights]] at international level.

==Early life==
Born in [[Sofia]], [[Bulgaria]], Havard's family moved to [[Bath, Somerset]] in England when he was six months old before relocating to [[Wigan]] a few years later.<ref>{{cite web |title=In focus: Ethan Havard |url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2020/09/16/in-focus-ethan-havard/ |website=Wigan Warriors |access-date=19 March 2024}}</ref> He played junior [[rugby league]] for [[Wigan St Patricks]], and was coached by his father, Keiron, who had a brief professional rugby league career with [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Super League: Wigan Warriors prospect Ethan Havard takes heed of rugby league lessons from his father |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12215/12378058/super-league-wigan-warriors-prospect-ethan-havard-takes-heed-of-rugby-league-lessons-from-his-father |website=Sky Sports |access-date=19 March 2024 |date=13 August 2021}}</ref>

==Career==
===Club===
In 2019 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] against [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921795|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors v Wakefield Trinity|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

Havard was charged with the Grade E offence of [[Eye-gouging|Eye Gouging]] for which he was sent off in the 79th minute of an Under-19s fixture against [[Newcastle Thunder]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-forward-facing-gouging-charge/|title=Wigan forward facing gouging charge|last=Darbyshire|first=Drew|date=2019-08-29|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2019-09-22}}</ref> He denied the offence, and after appearing in front of an independent disciplinary, was found to be 'not guilty'.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/wigan-warriors-young-forward-cleared-of-gouging-1-9973603|title=Wigan Warriors young forward cleared of gouging|website=www.wigantoday.net|language=en|access-date=2019-09-22}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Havard played for Wigan in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=BBC|date=26 May 2022 }}</ref>
On 14 October 2023, Havard was named as a substitute for Wigan, despite not being 100% fit, during their [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]]. He came off the bench (replacing Tyler Dupree at the time), but was substituted 10 minutes later as he suffered a hamstring injury.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>
On 8 June 2024, Havard played in Wigan's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] victory over Warrington from the interchange bench.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Havard played in Wigan's 9-2 2024 Super League grand final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Havard played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 30 May 2026, Havard played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship|title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===International===
Havard is eligible to represent [[Bulgaria national rugby league team|Bulgaria]] by place of birth. However he declared for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in 2020 ahead of his first call up for the [[England Knights]].<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/matty-ashton-and-ethan-havard-named-in-fresh-england-knights-squad/ | title=Matty Ashton and Ethan Havard named in fresh England Knights squad | date=3 March 2020 }}</ref>
On 29 April 2023, Havard made his England debut and scored a try as they defeated France 64–0 at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/65418274|title=England beat France 64-0 - hat-tricks for Handley and Williams|work=BBC Sport |date=28 April 2023 }}</ref>
